_comment: Windows EVTX/Sysmon golden for OTRF Security-Datasets compound APT3.
The dataset replicates MITRE ATT&CK Evaluations Round 1 APT3 activity and ships Windows telemetry plus an APT3 playbook.

Ground truth is scenario/playbook-derived from the OTRF dataset and ATT&CK evaluation scope; it is strong for log-correlation coverage but still must be verified from the staged telemetry at run time.

findings.description: APT3 emulation starts with script-based execution through Empire or Caldera stagers on Windows hosts
findings.artifact_hint: Windows Sysmon / PowerShell / process creation events from compound/windows/apt3

findings.description: Discovery activity enumerates system network configuration, owner or user, processes, services, groups, accounts, remote systems, and registry state
findings.artifact_hint: APT3 Empire resource files 12.A through 13.C and matching Windows event telemetry

findings.description: Credential-access phase includes keylogging or credential collection from files and password spraying
findings.artifact_hint: APT3 Empire resource files 15.A, 15.B, and 16.A plus Windows host events

findings.description: Lateral movement uses administrative shares, remote file copy, remote service execution, or WMI/DCOM execution between Windows hosts
findings.artifact_hint: APT3 resource files 16.B through 16.L and Sysmon process/network events

findings.description: Persistence or privilege escalation modifies Windows registry, scheduled tasks, or accessibility features
findings.artifact_hint: APT3 resource files 14.A, 17.A, and 17.C with registry/task/process telemetry

findings.description: Collection and exfiltration sequence stages files, compresses data, exfiltrates over an alternate protocol, and deletes artifacts
findings.artifact_hint: APT3 resource files 18.A through 19.D and Windows host/network telemetry
